bullets.text: Combined ratio was 93.6 for Q2, comfortably below the 96 target; April-May averaged near 92 while June rose to 97 due to Midwest flooding and weather catastrophes.
Progressive deployed its catastrophe response team on 19 occasions in Q2 alone, exceeding the prior annual record of 15 set in 2005, handling over 20,000 claims with 95% closed within 30 days.

Rising gas prices reduced miles driven and appear to be lowering future claims frequency; combined with potential mix shift from SUVs to smaller vehicles, creating a rapidly changing pricing environment.
Total written premium declined about 1% companywide; Personal Lines down 1% (Agency -3%, Direct +3%) and Commercial Auto down 5%, reflecting average premium reductions across all segments.
Policies in force were up 3% companywide: Agency auto down 2%, Direct auto up 7%, Special Lines up 8%, and Commercial Auto up 4% versus Q2 prior year.

Progressive classified $43 million of unrealized losses as other-than-temporary impairments, consistent with policy; management flagged continued vigilance required through year-end given market instability.
Internet-produced business remained the strongest source of new business growth; Agency new business lagged, especially in large states like New York and California where restrictions were imposed.
Concierge-level claims service continues to generate highest customer satisfaction scores; Net Promoter Score system is being used as a leading indicator of customer retention intent.
Progressive expanded its usage-based insurance program and tested the Name Your Price concept, both showing strong appeal; Massachusetts entry since May 1st attracted over 12,000 new customers.
New Chief Marketing Officer Larry Bloomenkranz joined Progressive; brand and advertising efforts showed improvements in awareness and recall metrics during the quarter.
